pois.name: Mount Maunganui Surf Beach
pois.short: Catch consistent beach breaks at one of NZ's most popular surf spots.
pois.commentary: The Main Beach at Mount Maunganui is a long, consistent beach break that works on all tides. The northern end near Moturiki Island offers more shelter for learners, while the open beach picks up more swell for experienced surfers. Multiple surf schools operate from the beachfront.
pois.audio_story: You have arrived at Mount Maunganui Main Beach, consistently rated one of the best surf beaches in New Zealand. The sand-bottom break stretches for over a kilometre, offering waves for every ability level. If you are learning, head to the northern end near Moturiki Island, where the rocky point provides shelter and the waves are smaller and more predictable. Experienced surfers should check the open beach, which picks up swell from the north and east and can produce head-high waves on a solid swell. The water here is noticeably warmer than much of New Zealand thanks to the East Auckland Current. After your session, the base track around Mauao, the extinct volcanic cone at the end of the beach, is a must-do walk with panoramic views of the coastline.

reviews.summary: The Mount Maunganui Surf Beach adventure draws rave reviews for the combination of adrenaline and spectacular scenery. Catch consistent beach breaks at one of NZ's most popular surf spots. Reviewers praise the professionalism of the operators and say the experience is worth every dollar for the memories it creates.

pois.name: Whakaari / White Island Volcano Tour
pois.short: View New Zealand's most active marine volcano by boat from Whakatane.
pois.commentary: Whakaari / White Island is New Zealand's most active cone volcano, sitting 48 km offshore from Whakatane. Boat tours offer views of the steaming crater and volcanic landscape from the water. Following the 2019 eruption, access protocols have changed significantly.
pois.audio_story: You are departing from Whakatane harbour towards Whakaari, White Island, New Zealand's most active marine volcano sitting 48 kilometres out in the Bay of Plenty. As the boat crosses the open water, you will see the island materialise from the haze, a raw, steaming mass of rock rising from the sea. The volcano has been erupting intermittently for at least 150,000 years, and the crater floor sits below sea level, making it geologically unique. The island's history includes attempts at sulphur mining in the early 1900s, with the ruins of the old factory still visible from the water. The 2019 eruption changed how visitors experience this place, and your guides will share the full story with deep respect for those affected. This is a humbling encounter with the raw power of the earth.

reviews.summary: The Whakaari / White Island Volcano Tour adventure draws rave reviews for the combination of adrenaline and spectacular scenery. View New Zealand's most active marine volcano by boat from Whakatane. Reviewers praise the professionalism of the operators and say the experience is worth every dollar for the memories it creates.

pois.name: McLaren Falls Glow Worm Kayak
pois.short: Paddle through a galaxy of glow worms on a night kayak at McLaren Falls.
pois.commentary: McLaren Falls Park hosts one of the Bay of Plenty's most magical experiences, a nighttime kayak through glow worm-lit waterways. Guided tours paddle along the lake and stream edges after dark, where thousands of Arachnocampa luminosa light up the overhanging banks. The park is 20 minutes from Tauranga.
pois.audio_story: You have arrived at McLaren Falls Park as the last light fades from the sky, which is exactly when the magic begins. Your guide will launch your kayak onto the still water and lead you quietly along the bush-fringed edges of the lake. As darkness settles, the overhanging banks come alive with thousands of glow worms, tiny Arachnocampa luminosa larvae casting their bioluminescent fishing lines. The effect is like floating through a galaxy. These are the same species found in Waitomo, but here you experience them from the open air, under a canopy of native bush with the Southern Cross overhead. Your guide will explain their fascinating and rather gruesome life cycle, each glowing dot is a hungry larva luring prey into sticky threads. Silence and slow paddling are rewarded with the brightest displays.

reviews.summary: The McLaren Falls Glow Worm Kayak adventure draws rave reviews for the combination of adrenaline and spectacular scenery. Paddle through a galaxy of glow worms on a night kayak at McLaren Falls. Reviewers praise the professionalism of the operators and say the experience is worth every dollar for the memories it creates.

pois.name: Tauranga Harbour Jet Boat
pois.short: Blast through Tauranga Harbour with 360-degree spins and high-speed drifts.
pois.commentary: Jet boat tours on Tauranga Harbour combine adrenaline thrills with harbour sightseeing. Boats reach speeds of up to 80 km/h with 360-degree spins, power slides, and high-speed runs through the harbour channels. Tours also pass the Port of Tauranga, one of Australasia's busiest.
pois.audio_story: You have arrived at the Tauranga waterfront, where a jet boat is idling with 600 horsepower waiting to be unleashed. Jet boats were invented right here in New Zealand by Sir William Hamilton, and this ride will show you exactly why the Kiwis perfected the concept. Your driver will rocket through the harbour channels at up to 80 kilometres per hour, executing 360-degree spins that drench everyone aboard and power slides that feel like the laws of physics have been temporarily suspended. Between the adrenaline bursts, you will cruise past the Port of Tauranga, one of the busiest ports in Australasia, and across the harbour towards the base of Mauao. The shallow-draught jet drive means the boat can operate in water just centimetres deep, opening up channels that no propeller-driven vessel could reach.

reviews.summary: The Tauranga Harbour Jet Boat adventure draws rave reviews for the combination of adrenaline and spectacular scenery. Blast through Tauranga Harbour with 360-degree spins and high-speed drifts. Reviewers praise the professionalism of the operators and say the experience is worth every dollar for the memories it creates.

pois.name: Papamoa Beach Stand-Up Paddleboarding
pois.short: Glide along the calm waters of Papamoa Beach on a stand-up paddleboard.
pois.commentary: Papamoa Beach offers flat, sheltered conditions ideal for stand-up paddleboarding, especially on calm mornings. Board hire is available from beachfront operators. The 16-kilometre stretch of golden sand provides plenty of room to explore.
pois.audio_story: You have arrived at Papamoa Beach, where 16 kilometres of golden sand and typically gentle surf create ideal conditions for stand-up paddleboarding. Early mornings here are sublime, the ocean goes glassy, the sun rises behind Mauao to the northwest, and you can paddle in solitude along one of New Zealand's longest uninterrupted beaches. SUP is deceptively great exercise, engaging your core with every stroke while you enjoy the view from an elevated vantage point above the water. You might spot stingrays in the shallows or dolphins cruising the outer break. Board hire is straightforward from operators along the beachfront, and first-timers will find the calm conditions here much more forgiving than ocean swells elsewhere.

reviews.summary: The Papamoa Beach Stand-Up Paddleboarding adventure draws rave reviews for the combination of adrenaline and spectacular scenery. Glide along the calm waters of Papamoa Beach on a stand-up paddleboard. Reviewers praise the professionalism of the operators and say the experience is worth every dollar for the memories it creates.

pois.name: Te Puke Quad Bike Adventures
pois.short: Ride quad bikes through kiwifruit country and native bush near Te Puke.
pois.commentary: Quad bike tours near Te Puke take riders through a mix of working farmland, kiwifruit orchards, and native bush tracks. Routes include river crossings, hill climbs, and bush trails with views of the Kaimai Range. No experience is necessary as guides provide full training.
pois.audio_story: You have arrived in Te Puke, the kiwifruit capital of the world, but today you are swapping orchards for off-road adventure. Your quad bike awaits on a working farm at the foot of the Kaimai Range, where the tracks wind through a landscape that shifts from open pasture to dense native bush and back again. Your guide will lead you through river crossings, up muddy hill climbs, and along ridgelines with panoramic views across the Bay of Plenty. No experience is required, the bikes are automatic, and your guide sets the pace. This region produces nearly 80 percent of New Zealand's kiwifruit, and you will ride right through the orchards to see the vines up close. Just be prepared to get muddy, that is half the fun.

reviews.summary: The Te Puke Quad Bike Adventures adventure draws rave reviews for the combination of adrenaline and spectacular scenery. Ride quad bikes through kiwifruit country and native bush near Te Puke. Reviewers praise the professionalism of the operators and say the experience is worth every dollar for the memories it creates.

pois.name: Ohope Beach Coasteering
pois.short: Scramble, swim, and leap off cliffs along the volcanic coastline at Ohope.
pois.commentary: Coasteering at Ohope involves scrambling along volcanic rocks, swimming through surges, and jumping from cliff platforms into deep water. The volcanic coastline near Whakatane provides natural platforms, channels, and deep pools ideal for this adventure sport.
pois.audio_story: You have arrived at Ohope, where the volcanic coastline creates a natural adventure playground for coasteering, the sport of scrambling, swimming, and jumping your way along a rocky shore. Your guide will kit you out with a wetsuit and helmet, then lead you along a route that weaves between rocky channels, surge pools, and cliff faces. The volcanic rock here has been eroded into platforms at various heights, providing natural jumping spots from 2 metres up to 8 metres for the truly committed. Between jumps, you will swim through rock channels where the ocean surges around you, scramble up seaweed-slicked rocks, and explore caves and blow holes carved into the headland. Coasteering is relatively new to New Zealand but has been hugely popular in the UK and Europe for years, and the Bay of Plenty's volcanic coastline is perfectly suited to it. No climbing or swimming expertise is needed, just a willingness to get wet and push your comfort zone.

reviews.summary: The Ohope Beach Coasteering adventure draws rave reviews for the combination of adrenaline and spectacular scenery. Scramble, swim, and leap off cliffs along the volcanic coastline at Ohope. Reviewers praise the professionalism of the operators and say the experience is worth every dollar for the memories it creates.
